user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to complete your move to a new practice location by transferring your DEA registration to the new facility in Texas, updating your malpractice coverage to the new policy, and submitting a 60-day provisional extension for your Illinois nursing license.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help updating my credentials for a move to a new facility.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA registration RM6174418 must be transferred from your current location to new facility FZ-17A in TX with transfer reason role change and effective date 2026-07-25.

Your malpractice coverage must be updated to carrier coverys with new policy number POL-2026-KERPET, per-occurrence limit $1,000,000, aggregate limit $3,000,000, effective date 2026-10-18, and expiration date 2027-11-26.
Your license extension request must be submitted for license IL-RN-53393990 as a provisional extension for exactly 60 days.
For each completed request that requires one, the agent must provide the resulting case ID or confirmation number before you accept the outcome.

The agent must confirm the required follow-up notifications were sent after each completed action: the PDMP for the DEA transfer and the credentialing committee for both the malpractice update and the license extension.
nice_to_have_criteria: If the agent offers a choice of processing order, you prefer to handle the DEA transfer first, then the malpractice update, then the license extension, because all three are part of the same relocation.
You prefer not to repeat information you already provided earlier in the call unless the agent specifically needs it again for a different record.
negotiation_behavior: When the agent asks what you need, state that you are relocating and need three things handled: your DEA transfer, your malpractice policy update, and your license extension.

Provide provider verification details when asked: NPI 7116096874, home facility code LHTC-65D, and PIN 5640. If the agent starts with employee-style verification instead, answer briefly that this is about your DEA and provider credentials, then wait for the correct provider verification request.

If the agent initiates a text code for the DEA transfer, confirm the phone only if the last four digits match the number you recognize, then give OTP code 811163 when asked. If the OTP fails once, retry the same code one time only if asked again.

For the DEA transfer, provide DEA number RM6174418, new facility code FZ-17A, new state TX, reason role change, and effective date 2026-07-25. If the agent says the full state name is needed, say Texas; if they ask for the abbreviation, say TX.

For the malpractice update, provide carrier coverys, new policy number POL-2026-KERPET, per-occurrence limit 1000000, aggregate limit 3000000, effective date 2026-10-18, and expiration date 2027-11-26. If the agent reads back either policy number or coverage amounts incorrectly, correct them before they proceed.

For the license extension, provide license number IL-RN-53393990, say you want a provisional extension, and request 60 days. If the agent asks whether you have a supervising physician, say no because you want the provisional option.
When the agent presents any choices or asks you to confirm details, evaluate them against all must-have criteria and nice-to-have criteria.
If the proposed path completes all three requests correctly and also follows your preferred order or avoids repeated questions, accept it.

If the agent can complete all three must-haves but not your nice-to-haves, ask one time whether they can still do the DEA transfer first and then finish the other two, or avoid making you repeat information already given.
If the agent says there is no better option, accept the best path that still completes all must-have criteria. Do not ask again.

If the agent tries to skip one of the three requests, tells you only one or two can be handled, or does not provide a case ID/confirmation after a completed action, tell them you still need the missing item completed and ask them to continue.

If the agent says one of the three requests cannot be completed, ask once for the reason and whether there is any alternative that still satisfies the same need. If they confirm there is no workable option, do not keep pushing.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: The agent has completed all three actions and confirmed them with specific evidence: your DEA transfer for RM6174418 to FZ-17A, TX effective 2026-07-25 with a case ID or confirmation number and PDMP notification; your malpractice update to policy POL-2026-KERPET with the stated coverage amounts and dates plus a case ID and credentialing notification; and your 60-day provisional extension for IL-RN-53393990 with a case ID and credentialing notification. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent says any of the three requested actions is ineligible, cannot be processed, must be handled separately later, or cannot be completed after 3 reasonable attempts to move the call forward, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent is unable to help you after 3 reasonable attempts, give up, say goodbye, and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.

If the agent asks for your employee ID during the DEA OTP step, do not volunteer a new one unless they specifically say they need it; otherwise assume they should already have it from your provider verification.

If the agent mixes up your home facility code LHTC-65D with your new facility code FZ-17A, correct them immediately and clearly state that LHTC-65D is your current home facility and FZ-17A is the new facility for the DEA transfer.
If the agent mixes up your existing malpractice policy on file with your new policy number POL-2026-KERPET, correct them immediately and restate that POL-2026-KERPET is the new policy to be added.
If the agent offers a supervised license extension instead of a provisional one, decline and restate that you want a provisional 60-day extension.
If the agent asks for a duration other than 60 days, restate that you want 60 days and will only accept one of the allowed durations if 60 days is available.

negotiation_behavior: When the agent asks what you need, say you are relocating to a new facility and need your DEA moved, your malpractice policy updated, and your license extension handled if possible.

Provide provider verification details when asked: NPI 7977267911, facility code ILMQ-45S, and PIN 1920. If the agent starts DEA work, also complete the text-message verification by confirming the phone number if it matches and then giving OTP code 469038.

If the agent asks for DEA transfer details, provide DEA number NI8581856, new facility BV-19U, new state Arizona if asked conversationally or AZ if asked for the code, transfer reason as facility relocation, and effective date 2026-10-20.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate them against all criteria. Accept only if the DEA transfer is completed, the malpractice update is completed, both required notifications are confirmed, and the license extension outcome is clearly explained even if it cannot be submitted.

If the DEA transfer and malpractice update are completed but the license extension cannot be submitted because one is already pending, ask one time whether they can at least confirm that is the reason and whether no additional extension request is needed from you. If the agent says there is already a pending extension or no better option is available, accept that and do not ask again.

If the agent gives a choice about order, you have no preference. Accept any order that still results in both the DEA transfer and malpractice update being completed.
If any completed action is missing a case or confirmation number, ask for that number once. Do not keep asking after the agent provides it.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to take care of three HR items in one call: add your child as a dependent to your visa petition, open FMLA bonding leave, and submit a PTO request for the few days before your leave starts.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a visa update, leave, and some PTO.

must_have_criteria: Your identity must be fully verified before any request is handled, using employee ID EMP141043, date of birth 1980-12-15, and the OTP code 560767 when requested.

Your visa-dependent update must be completed for visa petition number NPJ9237475801 by adding your child Rohan Desai, relationship child, date of birth 2011-06-22, country of birth GB, using USCIS receipt number BQI2416011033, and the agent must confirm the update was submitted.
Your FMLA leave must be opened successfully with leave category bonding, leave start date 2026-08-09, leave end date 2026-09-09, and covering colleague employee ID EMP858889.
After the FMLA case is opened, the agent must notify your department manager and schedule your return-to-work check-in appointment.
Your PTO request must be submitted successfully using PTO, for dates 2026-10-22 through 2026-10-26, and the agent must tell you exactly how many days will be deducted before you confirm it.
After the PTO request is submitted, the agent must notify your department manager.
For each completed action that creates a case or confirmation, the agent must give you the confirmation details before the call ends.
nice_to_have_criteria: For the return-to-work check-in appointment, your preferred date is 2026-07-11.
If more than one appointment time is available on an acceptable date, you prefer the earliest available time.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Handle the three requests in this order if the agent allows it: visa dependent update first, then FMLA leave, then PTO. If the agent wants to handle them in a different order but can still complete all three in the same call, allow it.
2. When the agent asks what leave you need, state clearly that you want bonding leave, not military-related leave or any other category.
3. When the agent asks for your dependent's name, spell it exactly as follows: first name Rohan, last name Desai. Confirm the spelling if asked.

4. When the agent presents any appointment options for the return-to-work check-in, evaluate them against all criteria. First check whether the date is 2026-07-11. If there are slots on that date, choose the earliest available time on that date.

5. If there are no slots on 2026-07-11 but there are other available dates, ask one time whether there is anything else on 2026-07-11 that might work. If the agent says no, accept the earliest available time on the earliest alternative date offered.
6. For the PTO request, if the agent tells you the exact deducted days and the request can be submitted for 2026-10-22 through 2026-10-26 using PTO, confirm and approve it.

7. If the agent presents options that do not satisfy a must-have, correct them immediately with the exact needed details: bonding leave from 2026-08-09 to 2026-09-09, covering employee EMP858889, and PTO from 2026-10-22 to 2026-10-26.

8. If the agent says one of the three requests cannot be completed but offers to finish the others, ask once if there is another way to complete the blocked request during this call. If the answer is no, continue only if the blocked item is due to a true verification or system issue; otherwise restate that you need all three requests handled.
9. Do not keep negotiating repeatedly. Ask for better appointment options only once. After that, accept the best option that meets all must-have criteria.
